details.summary: MCP.so only offers a manual web form at https://mcp.so/submit for listing submissions. No public API endpoint is documented for automated submissions, and there is no GitHub PR-based submission process. The GitHub repo at github.com/chatmcp/mcpso only hosts issues, not submissions. Submissions require signing in and filling out the form manually.
details.reason_not_automatable: No public API or GitHub PR submission mechanism available. Reverse-engineering the internal Next.js API route would be required, which is not officially supported.

listing_assets.name: my-automaton
listing_assets.short_description: my-automaton provides a ready-to-use MCP server for automation tasks, accessible at https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp, enabling AI agents to execute workflows programmatically.
listing_assets.description: my-automaton is a lightweight Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to bridge AI assistants with real-world automation. Hosted at https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp, it exposes a clean set of automation tools that AI agents can discover and invoke on the fly. Whether you need to orchestrate shell commands, manage files, or trigger scheduled workflows, my-automaton gives your AI copilot a structured, secure endpoint to get things done. Built with simplicity and extensibility in mind, it follows the MCP specification to ensure seamless integration with any compliant client.

manual_submission_steps: 5. Enter URL/GitHub: https://github.com/your-repo/my-automaton (or appropriate repo URL)
6. Enter MCP server config with endpoint: https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp
9. Add tags: mcp, automation, ai-tools, model-context-protocol, workflow, devtools, ai-agents, server
action_taken: Manual submission required. Result file documenting findings created at workspace/submission/mcp-so-result.json.
